title: Pairing, System Reasoning, Debugging, and Project Defense · Opportunity Brief: Pairing Contract
summary: A candidate treats collaboration as performance theater, accepts false premises, over-designs an abstract system, and describes a team project as entirely personal work. Predict the hiring decision, evidence quality, affected people, uncertainty, and ethical boundary before reviewing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pairing contract increment in multi-format technical defense packet. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and…
objectives: Establish roles, turn-taking, accessibility, tool use, communication, disagreement, and shared success for a collaborative exercise.
Evaluate take-home scope, ownership, confidentiality, accessibility, tool disclosure, deadline, compensation, and production-use risk before consent.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# job-search cumulative learner-controlled career campaign
# Browser labs review fictional evidence contracts only. They never contact people,
# submit applications, access accounts, expose personal data, negotiate, or accept offers.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-learner
target-role: [replace with role family, work outcomes, and bounded level]
jurisdiction: [replace with location and current official guidance boundary]
audience: [replace with reviewer need, time, format, and access context]
claim: [replace with one truthful role-relevant behavior claim]
evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, observation, changed case, contribution, and limit]
process-state: [replace with discover, investigate, apply, interview, offer, close, or archive]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
privacy-consent: [replace with purpose, minimization, permission, retention, deletion, and safe contact]
decision: [replace with learner-owned next decision and stop rule]
transfer-boundary: learner-approved external actions only
review:

steps.title: Predict Pairing Contract · Theory Pairing Contract
steps.instruction:
theory case — A candidate dominates a pair task, guesses at a bug, draws fashionable architecture, and claims a team project without separating personal work. The theory variation 4 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: defend one bounded career decision

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Pairing Contract contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Pairing Contract · Theory Pairing Contract.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for establish roles, turn-taking, accessibility, tool use, communication, disagreement, and shared success for a collaborative exercise. For Predict Pairing Contract · Theory Pairing Contract, keep Pairing Contract connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.text: A candidate dominates a pair task, guesses at a bug, draws fashionable architecture, and claims a team project without separating personal work. The theory variation 4 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: defend one bounded career decision

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Pairing Contract condition decided your prediction.

content.value: Web Content Accessibility Guidelines — Controls accessible portfolio and application artifacts while distinguishing automated checks from knowledgeable human and assistive-technology evaluation.

For Predict Pairing Contract · Theory Pairing Contract, challenge the documented misconception with changed-candidate, target-role, source-date, jurisdiction, audience, access, privacy, consent, process-state, interview, offer, rejection, repair, and learner-controlled transfer evidence: The strongest candidate should take control and finish alone.

options.text: The strongest candidate should take control and finish alone. That claim fails the predict pairing contract evidence boundary for Pairing Contract.

For predict pairing contract, apply Pairing Contract without its stated preconditions, omit the multi-format technical defense packet trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Pairing Contract for multi-format technical defense packet.
Establish roles, turn-taking, accessibility, tool use, communication, disagreement, and shared success for a collaborative exercise. Apply it to the concrete input and state the observable result.

content.text: Name learner goal, target role, jurisdiction, audience, claim, contribution, source date, evidence revision, accessibility, privacy, consent, process state, changed candidate, rejected failure, causal repair, decision owner, non-claim, and transfer limit; then calibrate the application or career decision to that exact evidence. content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A candidate dominates a pair task, guesses at a bug, draws fashionable architecture, and claims a team project without separating personal work. The worked example variation 4 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Establish roles, turn-taking, accessibility, tool use, communication, disagreement, and shared success for a collaborative exercise. A defensible solution applies Pairing Contract, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to multi-format technical defense packet.

content.code:
# Worked focus: career-pairing-contract — Establish roles, turn-taking, accessibility, tool use, communication, disagreement, and shared success for a collaborative exercise.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-candidate-4
focus: career-pairing-contract
decision-evidence: Establish roles, turn-taking, accessibility, tool use, communication, disagreement, and shared success for a collaborative exercise.
target-role: accessible software quality engineer
jurisdiction: example location requiring current official review
audience: technical reviewer with ten minutes and keyboard-only access
claim: one bounded role behavior is supported by a revision-linked changed case
evidence: repository case study, exact test result, contribution note, and limitation
process-state: investigate before applying
accessibility: structured text, reflow, visible focus, plain-language status, and equivalent format
privacy-consent: publish no sensitive person or third-party data; contact only with explicit consent
decision: proceed only after identity, role, evidence, access, and risk checks pass
verify: inspect links, extraction order, revision, changed case, disclosure, and source date
failure: reject invented impact, hidden assistance, exposed data, or unverified recruiter identity
repair: correct the first unsupported claim and add a changed-candidate regression
owner: learner controls every external action
transfer-boundary: manual learner-approved profile, application, conversation, interview, and offer decisions
content.caption: Runnable transformation example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: The strongest candidate should take control and finish alone. This fails the Pairing Contract contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG 2.2 Recommendation; ISO IEC 40500:2025) — Controls accessible portfolio and application artifacts while distinguishing automated checks from knowledgeable human and assistive-technology evaluation.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Establish roles, turn-taking, accessibility, tool use, communication, disagreement, and shared success for a collaborative exercise.
